title: Choosing between the district and state consumer commissions
p1: Most people file a consumer complaint in the wrong commission — and only realise it after weeks of delay. The confusing part is, the rules look similar on the surface, but the outcome depends entirely on where you file.
p2: A common misconception is that District Commission is only for ‘small’ cases and State Commission is just a higher appeal court. In reality, both are original courts depending on claim value, not hierarchy alone. This misunderstanding leads to rejected filings.
p3: Under the Consumer Protection Act, 2019, the deciding factor is the value of your claim. District Commissions handle cases up to ₹1 crore. State Commissions handle ₹1 crore to ₹10 crore. Filing in the wrong forum doesn’t strengthen your case — it delays it, sometimes forcing you to refile from scratch. The practical step is simple: first calculate your total claim value, then check where the opposite party is located, and file accordingly.
conclusion: If you had to file a claim today for a service failure worth ₹85 lakh, how would you decide the right commission without second-guessing yourself?
